accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and accurate context (DOE administers the program; 42 U.S.C. 18724 is the IIJA provision).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the FACA and FOIA references are accurate context for chapter 10 of title 5 and section 552.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill's purpose of reauthorizing and expanding the DOE Energy Sector Operational Support for Cyberresilience Program.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly states the bill amends Section 40125(c) of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes key conditions including the sole/unreviewable discretion, no-right-or-benefit, FACA nonapplicability, and disclosure exemptions.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the reauthorization period of fiscal years 2027 through 2031 replacing 2022 through 2026.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures all essential operative provisions: reauthorization, expanded purpose, ETAC, disclosure exemptions, and transaction authority.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent, following the bill's structure logically.

concise.why: Free of padding and repetition while covering the substance.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names DOE, the Secretary of Energy, the IIJA, FACA, and FOIA.

correct_figures.why: The fiscal year figures 2027-2031 and 2022-2026 are stated correctly.
